site.name: The Music Repository
seo.home.description: Public-domain scores, lessons, and songs for piano and guitar, paired with dozens of interactive practice tools you can use right in your browser.
seo.catalogue.description: Browse public-domain scores, songs, and lessons for piano and guitar — filter by type, genre, instrument, era, and skill level.
seo.collections.description: Guided collections, courses, and learning paths that carry you from fundamentals to fluency on piano and guitar.
seo.about.description: About The Music Repository — a growing public-domain library of lessons, songs, and interactive tools for piano and guitar.
seo.tools.description: Free interactive music tools — metronome, tuner, fretboard, circle of fifths, ear trainers, and more — right in your browser.
seo.drills.description: Objective practice drills for ear training, theory, and fretboard fluency, with scoring and spaced review.
seo.privacy.description: How The Music Repository collects, uses, and protects your personal information.
seo.terms.description: The terms of service governing your use of The Music Repository.
seo.cookies.description: How The Music Repository uses cookies and similar technologies.
seo.support.description: Support The Music Repository — help keep its public-domain lessons, songs, and interactive tools free and ad-free.
footer.tagline: One of the world's most comprehensive musical repositories for guitar and piano available.
footer.licenseNote: All content is public-domain or openly licensed (CC BY-SA). Attribution and license appear on every item.
footer.madeWith: Made for the love of music.
seo.faq.description: Answers to common questions about The Music Repository — getting started, practice, the interactive tools, and how the content is licensed.

faq.subtitle: Getting started, practice, the tools, and how the content works.
faq.empty: There are no questions here yet.
admin.faqSubtitle: Questions and answers shown on the public FAQ page.
support.title: Support The Music Repository
support.intro: The Music Repository is free, ad-free, and built around public-domain and openly-licensed music. If it helps your playing, a small tip on Ko-fi helps cover hosting and keeps new lessons, songs, and tools coming.
support.thanks: Every contribution is genuinely appreciated — thank you for keeping the music playing.
support.comingSoon: My Ko-fi page is being set up — please check back soon.
about.role: I'm a senior consultant, software engineer, and architect by day — and a musician by night. I sing, I've played guitar for twenty years, and I took up piano five years ago.
about.p1: I made The Music Repository out of convenience first. It started as a tool to help me make the most of my time learning piano and guitar, while also putting my skills as a technology professional to use. I kept building out more and more tools that I could share with the world, and as a result, I created this site.
about.p2: I want to create a genuine place where people of all levels can pick up and start learning to play an instrument. I have plans to expand this website with more content, more instruments, and more tools for players of every discipline and skill level. If you have an idea that could contribute to the development of The Music Repository, please {suggestion} and I'll do my best to consider and work on it.
about.p3: When I'm not writing code or playing, you'll usually find me climbing plastic mountains. I enjoy bouldering and fitness to keep healthy and keep the vibes going. Climbing and music have a lot of similarities when it comes to working on yourself in order to become a more competent musician/climber. There's so many parallels, but one has to be careful not to injure their fingers ;)
about.supportLead: If any of it has helped your playing, you can buy me a coffee on Ko-fi. Every little bit keeps new lessons, tools, and scores on the way.
about.closing: Thanks for being here — now go enjoy the wonderful world of music.

upgrade.note: Checkout runs through a payment provider (Stripe). Until API keys are provisioned this uses a mock checkout — no card, no charge — but the full flow (checkout → provider webhook → entitlement grant) is exercised end-to-end.

checkout.webhookNote: Clicking Pay simulates the provider firing its checkout.session.completed webhook to the API, which grants your premium entitlement — then returns you to the app.

admin.localizationDesc: Manage the site's interface text and translations.
flagadmin.title: Feature flags
flagadmin.subtitle: Toggle features per environment. Every change is recorded in the history.
flagadmin.targetingHelp: Leave blank for no targeting. Use the templates for a role gate or a percentage rollout.
flagadmin.invalidJson: Targeting must be valid JSON (or empty).
flagadmin.createBody: Create a runtime flag. It starts disabled in every environment. Code references it once shipped.
flagadmin.environmentsBody: Environments a flag can target. Deployments resolve by matching APP_ENV to a key.
flagadmin.deleteEnvBody: This removes the {key} environment and every flag's settings for it. Type {key} below to confirm.
flagadmin.deleteFlagBody: This deletes the {key} flag; it can be restored from the deleted list. Type {key} below to confirm.
flagadmin.importBody: Choose a JSON file (key → config, applied to every environment). Existing flags are updated.

transadmin.tipSave: Save the translations and publish them live
localeadmin.title: Localization
localeadmin.subtitle: Manage interface strings and content translations. Edits are saved as drafts; publish to make them live.
localeadmin.addStringBody: Enter a value for every locale — this creates the key across all of them.
localeadmin.localeHint: New locales are translatable here; wiring one into site routing + the language switcher is a separate deploy.
localeadmin.importBody: Upload a flat key → value JSON. Each value becomes a draft for the chosen locale.
localeadmin.importDone: Imported {count} strings — publish to make them live.
localeadmin.exportBody: Download a locale's strings as a key → value JSON.
localeadmin.badFile: Couldn't read that file — expected a flat key → value JSON object.
localeadmin.tipNewLocale: Register a new locale so its strings can be authored
localeadmin.tipImport: Import strings from a JSON file
localeadmin.tipExport: Export strings to a JSON file
localeadmin.editSubtitle: Manage this key's translations across locales.

localeadmin.tipPublish: Publish all drafts to the live site
localeadmin.tipEdit: Edit this key's translations across locales
localeadmin.deleteBody: This soft-deletes '{key}' — you can restore it later. Type the key to confirm.
